chatgpt.recommended_session_strategy: Use server-managed secure SameSite=Lax cookies with Capacitor configured to share cookies via the embedded webview loading the remote URL.
must_implement_now: Centralize API base URL resolution in a runtime helper
Ensure Capacitor webview shares cookies with remote web app
Validate SameSite cookie attributes for session continuity
Enhance AppUrlListener to parse OAuth return URLs minimally
Prioritize email code login stability in mobile shell
Add runtime environment detection for origin handling
should_avoid_now: Implementing custom token storage or token sprawl
Switching to native OAuth flows prematurely
Changing existing web app cookie/session behavior
Overbuilding OAuth return handling with complex schemes
continuity_risks: Cookies not shared properly between Capacitor webview and backend
API calls using inconsistent origins causing CORS or session loss
OAuth redirects breaking due to missing or incorrect deep link handling
Session expiration or invalidation not detected in mobile shell
Unstable alternate login options causing user confusion
recommended_test_cases: Mobile app login with email code maintains session after app restart
API calls from mobile shell use correct centralized API base URL
OAuth redirect URLs trigger minimal AppUrlListener handling without errors
Web app login and session behavior unchanged after mobile changes
Cookie SameSite and Secure flags verified on Android and iOS
Session continuity verified across app background/foreground cycles
implementation_order: Centralize API base URL resolution in a shared runtime helper
Verify and configure Capacitor webview cookie sharing with backend
Test and confirm server-set cookies have proper SameSite and Secure flags
Enhance AppUrlListener to minimally parse OAuth returns without full flow
Add runtime environment detection to toggle origin handling logic
Stabilize email code login UI and flows in mobile shell
chatgpt.one_sentence_summary: Stabilize mobile login by centralizing API origin handling, ensuring cookie-based session continuity in Capacitor webview, and preparing minimal OAuth return parsing without disrupting web app behavior.

grok.recommended_session_strategy: Use server-managed HTTP-only cookies with SameSite=None and explicit domain/secure flags, served from a stable public origin that both web and Capacitor remote loads can reach.
must_implement_now: Add runtime getApiBase() helper that returns public origin for native and same-origin for web
Update tRPC client to always use the helper for base URL
Ensure Set-Cookie headers include SameSite=None; Secure; Domain=.auditapatron.com
Strengthen AppUrlListener to route code-login deep links directly to existing auth flow
Add Capacitor server config to allow cleartext localhost only in dev
Document cookie domain and origin env vars for staging/prod
should_avoid_now: Introducing client-side token storage or refresh logic
Changing any existing web login routes or cookie behavior
Implementing full OAuth browser flows or custom schemes
Rewriting auth middleware or session store
continuity_risks: Cookie not set when webview loads remote URL on different subdomain
iOS ATS blocking non-HTTPS calls in production builds
Session loss on app background/foreground if cookie domain mismatches
Deep-link code handling colliding with existing Wouter routes
recommended_test_cases: Fresh install on Android/iOS completes email code login and retains session after restart
Web login continues to work unchanged on desktop and mobile browsers
App survives background/foreground and network switch without re-auth
API calls succeed from both same-origin web and native remote load
Invalid/expired session returns 401 and triggers minimal re-login prompt
implementation_order: Introduce and wire centralized getApiBase() helper
Update tRPC/http client to use the helper everywhere
Adjust cookie attributes on the Express session/cookie layer
Harden AppUrlListener for auth deep links
Add dev-only cleartext exception and prod HTTPS enforcement
Run the five test cases on both platforms
grok.one_sentence_summary: Centralize API origin resolution and lock down cookie attributes to deliver reliable cookie-based sessions for the remote-loaded Capacitor app while leaving the web auth stack untouched.

gemini.recommended_session_strategy: Leverage secure, HTTP-only, SameSite=None; Secure server-managed cookies for session continuity within the Capacitor webview.
must_implement_now: Centralize API client base URL configuration
Configure backend SameSite=None; Secure for session cookies
Verify backend CORS headers for Capacitor origin
Enhance AppUrlListener for custom scheme redirects
Implement robust API error handling in frontend
Ensure webview session persistence across app restarts
should_avoid_now: Implementing native-specific token storage for webview sessions
Deep integration of Capacitor plugins for auth (unless critical)
Overhauling the web app's existing login UI
continuity_risks: SameSite cookie misconfiguration for cross-origin webview
CORS misconfigurations between webview and API
Deep link handling failures on various OS versions
Inconsistent API base URL resolution across environments
Session expiration handling leading to poor UX
recommended_test_cases: Successful email code login and session persistence
API calls work correctly after login and app restart
Deep link handling for custom scheme (e.g., mock OAuth return)
Web app login and session continuity (ensure no breakage)
Network connectivity changes during an active session
implementation_order: 1. Centralize API client base URL configuration
2. Configure backend SameSite=None; Secure for session cookies
3. Verify backend CORS headers for Capacitor origin
4. Test existing email code login in Capacitor
5. Enhance AppUrlListener for custom scheme handling
6. Document future OAuth integration points for phase 2
gemini.one_sentence_summary: Stabilize mobile login by centralizing API origin, ensuring correct SameSite cookie configuration, and preparing for custom scheme OAuth returns, all while preserving the existing web architecture.
